chapterSummary: The Dursleys, obsessed with normalcy, are increasingly disturbed by strange occurrences and whispers of the Potters, whom they despise. Meanwhile, Albus Dumbledore and Minerva McGonagall arrive on Privet Drive, revealing the death of Lily and James Potter and the miraculous survival of their son, Harry, at the hands of Voldemort. They prepare to leave baby Harry on the Dursleys' doorstep, setting the stage for his unusual upbringing.

locations.name: Privet Drive
locations.description: A perfectly normal, neat, and tidy suburban street, the last place one would expect strange occurrences.
lore: Represents the mundane, non-magical world the Dursleys inhabit.
The setting for the initial magical intrusions into the Muggle world.

locations.name: Number Four, Privet Drive
locations.description: The Dursleys' meticulously normal house, a bastion against anything strange or mysterious.
lore: The Dursleys' home, where they guard their secret about the Potters.
Becomes Harry Potter's unwilling childhood home.

threads.description: The Dursleys' desperate attempt to maintain normalcy and hide their connection to the magical world.
threads.development: The Dursleys' carefully constructed normal world is increasingly threatened by strange events and whispers of the Potters, forcing them to confront their hidden fears.

threads.description: The mystery surrounding the strange events (owls, shooting stars, cloaked people) and their connection to the magical world.
threads.development: The chapter introduces a series of inexplicable phenomena observed by Muggles and discussed by wizards, hinting at a major magical event.

threads.description: The fate of the Potters and their son, Harry, after Voldemort's attack.
threads.development: Rumors of Voldemort's defeat and the Potters' deaths are confirmed, and Harry's miraculous survival is revealed, along with the plan for his future.

scenes.summary: On a seemingly ordinary Tuesday morning, the Dursleys, a family obsessed with their own normalcy, begin their day. Mr. Dursley hums, Mrs. Dursley gossips, and Dudley throws a tantrum, all while an unnoticed tawny owl flutters past their window. The narrator reveals their deepest fear: the secret of their connection to the 'unDursleyish' Potters and their son.

threadMutations.threadDescription: The Dursleys' desperate attempt to maintain normalcy and hide their connection to the magical world.

knowledgeMutations.content: His family's secret fear of the Potters being discovered.
Her family's secret fear of the Potters being discovered.

scenes.summary: Mr. Dursley leaves for work, his mind on drills, but his routine is disrupted by the sight of a cat reading a map. He dismisses it as a trick of light, but the cat continues to observe him, its behavior increasingly peculiar. This initial encounter plants a seed of unease in his perfectly normal day.

threadMutations.threadDescription: The Dursleys' desperate attempt to maintain normalcy and hide their connection to the magical world.
The mystery surrounding the strange events (owls, shooting stars, cloaked people) and their connection to the magical world.

knowledgeMutations.content: The observation of a cat behaving unnaturally (reading a map, then a sign).

scenes.summary: Throughout his workday, Mr. Dursley is increasingly unnerved by the proliferation of strangely dressed people in cloaks, whispering excitedly. He overhears the name 'Potter' and 'Harry' and is gripped by fear, attempting to call his wife before dismissing his worries. Later, an old man in a violet cloak hugs him, proclaiming 'You-Know-Who has gone' and calling him a 'Muggle,' further shattering his sense of normalcy. At home, a news report confirms widespread unusual animal behavior and shooting stars, solidifying his growing dread.

relationshipMutations.type: Growing tension and fear over shared secret

scenes.summary: Back home, Mr. Dursley nervously broaches the subject of Mrs. Dursley's estranged sister, Petunia, and the strange events. Mrs. Dursley, though angry, confirms their nephew's name is Harry. Mr. Dursley's unease persists as he sees the same tabby cat still waiting outside, its gaze fixed on Privet Drive, hinting at an impending, unavoidable confrontation with the world they so desperately try to ignore.

threadMutations.threadDescription: The Dursleys' desperate attempt to maintain normalcy and hide their connection to the magical world.

knowledgeMutations.content: She confirms her nephew's name is Harry.

scenes.summary: As Mr. Dursley finally drifts to an uneasy sleep, Albus Dumbledore appears silently on Privet Drive, extinguishing streetlights with a 'Put-Outer'. He is joined by Professor McGonagall, who transforms from the tabby cat. They discuss the death of Lily and James Potter and the miraculous survival of their son, Harry, at the hands of Voldemort. Hagrid then arrives on a flying motorcycle, carrying the infant Harry, bearing a lightning-bolt scar. Despite McGonagall's protests, Dumbledore firmly places the sleeping baby, along with a letter, on the Dursleys' doorstep, leaving him to an uncertain future.
